summary: The implementation covers the majority of requirements for the social media platform. Most backend data models, functions, and frontend components are present. However, there are notable gaps: the User data model is incomplete, the Reels feature appears to be missing a dedicated data model, Stories have some implementation but lack backend functions for creation and filtering, and several auxiliary features like following and profile editing are not explicitly verified.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement User data model in Motoko backend with fields: id (Principal), username (Text), email (Text), and subscription (Bool)
missing_requirements.reason: The backend implements UserProfile type with username, email, and subscription fields, but it lacks the 'id (Principal)' field as specified. The requirement explicitly states the User type should have an 'id' field of type Principal, but UserProfile omits this.

missing_requirements.requirement: Create frontend StoryViewer component with carousel navigation that displays stories with media content and auto-advance timer
missing_requirements.reason: StoryViewer component is partially implemented with navigation and auto-advance, but the acceptance criteria requires support for 'tap navigation (left/right)'. The current implementation uses full-click navigation (handleTap checks left third vs rest), which may not match the expected tap interface.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement backend data model for Stories with fields: id (Nat), author (Principal), mediaUrl (Text), expiresAt (Nat timestamp), and views (array of Principal)
missing_requirements.reason: The backend Story type uses 'media: ?Storage.ExternalBlob' instead of 'mediaUrl (Text)' as specified. The requirement explicitly states mediaUrl should be of type Text, but the implementation uses a different storage approach.

hallucinated_features.description: Implementation of Stripe configuration and checkout functionality beyond basic subscription
hallucinated_features.reason: The diff includes StripeSetup component, useSetStripeConfiguration, useIsStripeConfigured hooks, and detailed Stripe integration logic. While REQ-10 requires Stripe integration for subscriptions, the implementation includes admin-level Stripe configuration UI and extensive Stripe management features not specified in requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: Admin authorization and access control system
hallucinated_features.reason: The implementation includes AdminGuard component, useIsCallerAdmin hook, and extensive access control logic in backend with AccessControl module and MixinAuthorization. No requirement specifies admin roles, permissions, or access control features.

hallucinated_features.description: Blob storage system with external storage capabilities
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend includes Storage and MixinStorage modules with ExternalBlob functionality and upload progress tracking. While media storage is implied by mediaUrl fields in requirements, the implementation of a full blob storage system with external storage and upload progress exceeds the specified requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: Payment success and failure dedicated pages
hallucinated_features.reason: The routing configuration includes PaymentSuccess and PaymentFailure page routes. REQ-10 only specifies 'User is redirected back to app with success message' after payment, not dedicated success/failure pages.

The implementation includes useMarkNotificationAsRead hook and notification read/unread UI management. REQ-18 specifies tracking read status in the data model, but REQ-19 only requires fetching notifications, not marking them as read.
